bracket._doc: Optional per-report bracket override. Add this as a top-level 'bracket' block in the report's analyzer template (alongside 'main' / 'ana' / 'web'); the analyzer copies it to settings.bracket, where the bracket view reads it. The reconstructed bracket auto-detects group stages / playoffs from match data; use these only to force the shape when detection gets a specific event wrong. Every key is optional.
bracket._doc_overrides: Force a series winner (tech loss / mis-scored final). Key = series key or tag, value = winning team id. The real map score is kept; the result just shows as overridden.
bracket._doc_stages: Force the phase/format shape. An ordered array, one entry per stage. Each entry is an object {kind, format, teams, groups} or a short 'kind[:format[:teams[:groups]]]' string. kind = group | bracket | none. format = rr | swiss | de | se. Bare 'de'/'se'/'rr'/'swiss' is shorthand. Add until/from/dates to an object stage to split strictly by calendar when phases share a roster and run back-to-back.
bracket._doc_divisions: Force a parallel-division split detection misses. Name => filter. teams = team ids in the division; days = weekdays it plays; dates = 'A..B' range; qualifies_to = shared meta event the winners advance to.
bracket._doc_months: Force (true) or suppress (false) the month-by-month performance grid shown for long, overlapping multi-event seasons.

_doc_precomputed_bracket: DIFFERENT feature / different key. The block above is the forcing config (report.settings.bracket). A fully precomputed bracket instead goes on report.bracket (top level), and the view renders it AS-IS — no detection, no match data needed. Compact webapi-like shape shown below under '_report_bracket'. Series: {teams:[idA,idB], score:[a,b], bo?, mids?(match ids for the popup), date?, winner?(else inferred from score)}. Stages: group {standings:[{team,w,d,l,mw,ml}], format} or bracket {upper/lower:[{name,series}], grand_final:[series]}.
